events.label: Bash
events.text:
cd /app/media && python3 -c "
import wave, numpy as np
w = wave.open('audio_engine_006.wav','rb')
print(w.getparams())
fs = w.getframerate()
n = w.getnframes()
data = w.readframes(n)
import numpy as np
arr = np.frombuffer(data, dtype=np.int16)
print('channels', w.getnchannels())
if w.getnchannels()>1:
    arr = arr.reshape(-1, w.getnchannels())
    arr = arr.mean(axis=1)
print(len(arr), fs, len(arr)/fs)
"

## Report

**1. Bash tool status — CRITICAL FINDING: confirmed broken, matches your report exactly.**

So this is not a fluke of my session or a one-off transient error; it's a systemic failure of command execution in this environment. No…
